  • Hide All Code

EMG Fatigue Analysis Pipeline

Morgan Virolan

2026-02-20

1 Scientific question and Objectives

This is the companion notebook for the AMSTROM Campain, where we analyze the sEMG and torque data from the fatigue protocol. It’s main objectives is (at this point) to quntifify the fatigue-induced changes in sEMG features (RMS, MDF) during the dynamic (EX_DYN) and static (EX_STA) fatigue sequences, and to see how they relate to the torque loss. It’s the first step of the broader study which will also include the analysis of other physiological signals recorded during the same experimental protocole (BIA, NIRS, Mecanical values) + stats & ML.

Dataset structure Signal assumptions (delsys hardware band-pass ?? “slow” ramp up -> cut middle sequence + fist windows on STA)

2 Data and Preprocessing Framework

library(arrow)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(tibble)
library(ggplot2)
library(signal)   # butter(), filtfilt()
library(glue)


options(dplyr.summarise.inform = FALSE)
RUN_ID <- "101SaCl_20251127"
subject_directory <- "results/DATA_EXPORT" |> 
  file.path(RUN_ID) |>
  glue::glue()

library(arrow)
library(dplyr)

master_file_path <- file.path(subject_directory, "02_master_index_grid.parquet")
emg_file_path    <- file.path(subject_directory, "02_emg_compact.parquet")
torque_file_path <- file.path(subject_directory, "02_torque_compact.parquet")

master_table <- read_parquet(master_file_path)
emg_table    <- read_parquet(emg_file_path)
torque_table <- read_parquet(torque_file_path)

analysis_table <- master_table |>
  inner_join(
    emg_table |> select(-VC, -VC_count, -SEQ, -SEQ_index),
    by = "time_index"
  ) |>
  left_join(
    torque_table |> select(-VC, -VC_count, -SEQ, -SEQ_index),
    by = "time_index"
  )

cat("nrow(master_table):", nrow(master_table), "\n")
## nrow(master_table): 5410994
cat("nrow(emg_table):   ", nrow(emg_table), "\n")
## nrow(emg_table):    5410994
cat("nrow(torque_table):", nrow(torque_table), "\n")
## nrow(torque_table): 1306102
cat("nrow(analysis_table):", nrow(analysis_table), "\n")
## nrow(analysis_table): 5410994
## master & emg must share the same hz, torque is expected to be about 1/5 of that and do contain n/a
analysis_table %>%
  summarise(
    duration_s = max(time_ref_s) - min(time_ref_s),
    fs_est_hz = n() / duration_s,
    torque_na_frac = mean(is.na(torque_raw))
  )
## # A tibble: 1 × 3
##   duration_s fs_est_hz torque_na_frac
##        <dbl>     <dbl>          <dbl>
## 1      2519.     2148.          0.759
# MVC_REF = 3 to 4 reps of maximal voluntary contraction (MVC) at the start of the session
mvc_ref_table <- analysis_table |>
  dplyr::filter(.data[["SEQ"]] == "MVC_REF",
              .data[["VC"]] == 1) |> # only keep actual reps (voluntary contraction = 1) / no rest
  dplyr::arrange(.data[["time_index"]])


#SVC_REF = submaximal voluntary contractions at 30/50/70% of MVC, used for calibration of torque and EMG
svc_ref_table <- analysis_table |>
  dplyr::filter(.data[["SEQ"]] == "SVC_REF",
              .data[["VC"]] == 1) |> 
  dplyr::arrange(.data[["time_index"]])


#MVC_RECOV_DYN = MVC checkup after the dynamic fatigue sequence (EX_DYN) + 5 mins rest -  used to assess recovery oafter fatigue

mvc_recov_dyn_table <- analysis_table |>
  dplyr::filter(.data[["SEQ"]] == "MVC_RECOV_DYN",
              .data[["VC"]] == 1) |> 
  dplyr::arrange(.data[["time_index"]])


#MVC_RECOV_STA = MVC checkup after the static fatigue sequence (EX_STA) + 5 mins rest -  used to assess recovery after fatigue

mvc_recov_sta_table <- analysis_table |>
  dplyr::filter(.data[["SEQ"]] == "MVC_RECOV_STA",
              .data[["VC"]] == 1) |> 
  dplyr::arrange(.data[["time_index"]])
## EX_DYN = dynamic fatigue sequence (62 reps, 3s efforts 2s recup, max effort each rep)
ex_dyn_table <- analysis_table |>
  dplyr::filter(.data[["SEQ"]] == "EX_DYN",
                .data[["VC"]] == 1) |>
  dplyr::arrange(.data[["time_index"]])

## EX_STA = static fatigue sequence (1 rep, 60s effort, 70% of baseline mvc)
ex_sta_table <- analysis_table |>
  dplyr::filter(.data[["SEQ"]] == "EX_STA",
                .data[["VC"]] == 1) |> #just in case sequencing is loose (everything should be VC==1)
  dplyr::arrange(.data[["time_index"]])

2.1 Dataset Structure

2.2 Torque Calibration

#set values, from the biodex calibration
torque_offset_volt <- 0.106
torque_gain_Nm_per_volt <- 145.30

calc_torque_nm <- function(df) {
  df %>% dplyr::mutate(torque_Nm = torque_gain_Nm_per_volt * (torque_raw - torque_offset_volt))
}

ex_dyn_table       <- calc_torque_nm(ex_dyn_table)
ex_sta_table       <- calc_torque_nm(ex_sta_table)
mvc_ref_table      <- calc_torque_nm(mvc_ref_table)
svc_ref_table      <- calc_torque_nm(svc_ref_table)
mvc_recov_dyn_table <- calc_torque_nm(mvc_recov_dyn_table)
mvc_recov_sta_table <- calc_torque_nm(mvc_recov_sta_table)

#print the 10 lines of each + 10 lines of the raw table to see the difference
print(head(ex_dyn_table |> dplyr::select(time_ref_s, torque_raw, torque_Nm), 10))
## # A tibble: 10 × 3
##    time_ref_s torque_raw torque_Nm
##         <dbl>      <dbl>     <dbl>
##  1      1090.      0.473      53.4
##  2      1090.     NA          NA  
##  3      1090.     NA          NA  
##  4      1090.     NA          NA  
##  5      1090.      0.481      54.6
##  6      1090.     NA          NA  
##  7      1090.     NA          NA  
##  8      1090.     NA          NA  
##  9      1090.      0.489      55.6
## 10      1090.     NA          NA

CLEANUP

#cleanup raw torque (keep only the calibrated torque in Nm to save memory)
# ex_dyn_table <- ex_dyn_table |> dplyr::select(-torque_raw)
# ex_sta_table <- ex_sta_table |> dplyr::select(-torque_raw)

# mvc_recov_sta_table <- mvc_recov_sta_table |> dplyr::select(-torque_raw)

print(head(ex_dyn_table, 10))

##    time_index time_ref_s SEQ_index SEQ       VC VC_count sequence_valid.x

##  2    2342305      1090.         7 EX_DYN     1        1                1
##  3    2342306      1090.         7 EX_DYN     1        1                1
##  4    2342307      1090.         7 EX_DYN     1        1                1
##  5    2342308      1090.         7 EX_DYN     1        1                1
##  6    2342309      1090.         7 EX_DYN     1        1                1
##  7    2342310      1090.         7 EX_DYN     1        1                1
##  8    2342311      1090.         7 EX_DYN     1        1                1
##  9    2342312      1090.         7 EX_DYN     1        1                1
## 10    2342313      1090.         7 EX_DYN     1        1                1
## # ℹ 11 more variables: period_s <dbl>, run_id.x <chr>, emg6 <dbl>, emg8 <dbl>,
## #   emg10 <dbl>, sequence_valid.y <int>, run_id.y <chr>, torque_raw <dbl>,
## #   sequence_valid <int>, run_id <chr>, torque_Nm <dbl>
#remove the analysis table  - we extracted what we need for now
rm(analysis_table)
rm(emg_table)
rm(torque_table)
gc()
##            used  (Mb) gc trigger   (Mb)  max used   (Mb)
## Ncells  1153822  61.7    2182532  116.6   1751894   93.6
## Vcells 35933324 274.2  161129707 1229.4 201412133 1536.7

2.3 Signal Processing

# CONSTANTS
emg_sampling_frequency_hz <- 2148.149

emg_bandpass_low_hz <- 20
emg_bandpass_high_hz <- 450
emg_filter_order <- 4


# BANDPASS FILTER FUNCTION -----------------------------------
# clean artefacts, movement related noises, high freq noise.
emg_bandpass_filter <- function(emg_signal, sampling_frequency_hz, 
                                low_hz, high_hz, filter_order) {
  
  # Centers signal around zero by subtracting the mean.
  # This prevents a large "spike" at 0Hz in the frequency domain (dc offset removal)
  emg_centered <- emg_signal - mean(emg_signal, na.rm = TRUE)
  
  # Nyquist Normalization : cutoffs needed for digital filters
  # ratio of the Nyquist frequency (half the sampling rate).
  normalized_cutoffs <- c(low_hz, high_hz) / (sampling_frequency_hz / 2)
  
  # Butterworth filter 
  butter_filter <- signal::butter(filter_order, normalized_cutoffs, type = "pass")
  
  # Zero-Phase Filtering
  # filter twice (forward and backward) -> no time-shift (phase lag
  signal::filtfilt(butter_filter, emg_centered)
}

3 Definition of the Analysis Window

Adds markdown later to justify windows (comparable, avoid artefacts dues to ramps, enough hz to still run everything, etc)

3.1 Contraction Labels

3.2 Middle 1 s Window Extraction

extract_middle_1s_window <- function(rep_df, window_length_s = 1.0) {

  rep_df |>
    dplyr::group_by(.data$VC_count) |>
    dplyr::mutate(
      t_mid = (min(.data$time_ref_s) + max(.data$time_ref_s)) / 2,
      mid_start = .data$t_mid - window_length_s / 2,
      mid_end   = .data$t_mid + window_length_s / 2
    ) |>
    dplyr::filter(
      .data$time_ref_s >= mid_start,
      .data$time_ref_s < mid_end
    ) |>
    dplyr::select(-.data$t_mid, -.data$mid_start, -.data$mid_end) |>
    dplyr::ungroup()
}
#mvc_ref
mvc_ref_mid1s_table <- extract_middle_1s_window(mvc_ref_table)
## Warning: Use of .data in tidyselect expressions was deprecated in tidyselect 1.2.0.
## ℹ Please use `"t_mid"` instead of `.data$t_mid`
## This warning is displayed once every 8 hours.
## Call `lifecycle::last_lifecycle_warnings()` to see where this warning was
## generated.
## Warning: Use of .data in tidyselect expressions was deprecated in tidyselect 1.2.0.
## ℹ Please use `"mid_start"` instead of `.data$mid_start`
## This warning is displayed once every 8 hours.
## Call `lifecycle::last_lifecycle_warnings()` to see where this warning was
## generated.
## Warning: Use of .data in tidyselect expressions was deprecated in tidyselect 1.2.0.
## ℹ Please use `"mid_end"` instead of `.data$mid_end`
## This warning is displayed once every 8 hours.
## Call `lifecycle::last_lifecycle_warnings()` to see where this warning was
## generated.
mvc_ref_mid1s_table |>
  dplyr::group_by(VC_count) |>
  dplyr::summarise(
    n_rows = dplyr::n(),
    duration_s = max(time_ref_s) - min(time_ref_s),
    fs_est = n_rows / duration_s,
    .groups = "drop"
  )
## # A tibble: 3 × 4

##      <int>  <int>      <dbl>  <dbl>
## 1        1   2149      1.000  2149.
## 2        2   2149      1.000  2149.
## 3        3   2148      0.999  2149.
#svc_ref
svc_ref_mid1s_table <- extract_middle_1s_window(svc_ref_table)

svc_ref_mid1s_table |>
  dplyr::group_by(VC_count) |>
  dplyr::summarise(
    n_rows = dplyr::n(),
    duration_s = max(time_ref_s) - min(time_ref_s),
    fs_est = n_rows / duration_s,
    .groups = "drop"
  )

## 3        3   2149      1.000  2149.
#mvc_recov_dyn
mvc_recov_dyn_mid1s_table <- extract_middle_1s_window(mvc_recov_dyn_table)

mvc_recov_dyn_mid1s_table |>
  dplyr::group_by(VC_count) |>
  dplyr::summarise(
    n_rows = dplyr::n(),
    duration_s = max(time_ref_s) - min(time_ref_s),
    fs_est = n_rows / duration_s,
    .groups = "drop"
  )

mvc_recov_sta_mid1s_table <- extract_middle_1s_window(mvc_recov_sta_table)

mvc_recov_sta_mid1s_table |>
  dplyr::group_by(VC_count) |>

#ex_dyn
ex_dyn_mid1s_table <- extract_middle_1s_window(ex_dyn_table)

ex_dyn_mid1s_table |>
  dplyr::group_by(VC_count) |>
  dplyr::summarise(
    n_rows = dplyr::n(),

## # A tibble: 62 × 4
##    VC_count n_rows duration_s fs_est
##       <int>  <int>      <dbl>  <dbl>
##  1        1   2149      1.000  2149.
##  2        2   2148      0.999  2149.
##  3        3   2148      0.999  2149.
##  4        4   2148      0.999  2149.
##  5        5   2148      0.999  2149.

## # ℹ 52 more rows
# EX_STA window grid (1 s, non-overlap)

window_length_s <- 1.0 #must match
window_step_s   <- 1.0

t_start <- min(ex_sta_table$time_ref_s)
t_end   <- max(ex_sta_table$time_ref_s)

window_start_s <- seq(t_start, t_end - window_length_s, by = window_step_s)

ex_sta_window_table <- tibble::tibble(
  window_id      = seq_along(window_start_s),
  window_start_s = window_start_s,
  window_end_s   = window_start_s + window_length_s,
  window_mid_s   = window_start_s + window_length_s / 2
)

nrow(ex_sta_window_table)
## [1] 60
ex_sta_window_table |> dplyr::slice(1:5)
## # A tibble: 5 × 4
##   window_id window_start_s window_end_s window_mid_s
##       <int>          <dbl>        <dbl>        <dbl>
## 1         1          2067.        2068.        2067.
## 2         2          2068.        2069.        2068.
## 3         3          2069.        2070.        2069.
## 4         4          2070.        2071.        2070.
## 5         5          2071.        2072.        2071.
## attach data

ex_sta_mid1s_table <- ex_sta_table |>
  dplyr::mutate(
    window_id = floor((time_ref_s - t_start) / window_step_s) + 1L
  ) |>
  dplyr::filter(
    window_id >= 1L,
    window_id <= nrow(ex_sta_window_table)
  ) |>
  dplyr::left_join(
    ex_sta_window_table,
    by = "window_id"
  ) |>
  dplyr::select(
    time_index, time_ref_s, SEQ_index, SEQ, VC, VC_count,
    window_id, window_start_s, window_end_s, window_mid_s,
    emg6, emg8, emg10, torque_raw, torque_Nm
  )

# quick check: should show ~1.0 s duration per window
ex_sta_mid1s_table |>
  dplyr::group_by(window_id) |>
  dplyr::summarise(
    n_rows = dplyr::n(),
    duration_s = max(time_ref_s) - min(time_ref_s),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) |>
  dplyr::slice(1:10) |>
  print()
## # A tibble: 10 × 3
##    window_id n_rows duration_s
##        <dbl>  <int>      <dbl>
##  1         1   2149      1.000
##  2         2   2148      0.999
##  3         3   2148      0.999
##  4         4   2148      0.999
##  5         5   2148      0.999
##  6         6   2148      0.999
##  7         7   2149      1.000
##  8         8   2148      0.999
##  9         9   2148      0.999
## 10        10   2148      0.999

4 Feature Extraction

Markdown explications + features etc. All EMG features are computed on previously defined 1 s windows.

4.1 EMG Amplitude Descriptor (RMS)

# RMS FUNCTION -----------------------------------
# Squares every point (-> everything becomes positive), finds average, takes the root -> power of the signal (same unit). amplitude/intensity of the contraction.
emg_rms <- function(emg_signal) {
  sqrt(mean(emg_signal^2, na.rm = TRUE))
}

4.2 EMG Spectral Descriptor (Median Frequency)

markdown explications

# MDF

emg_median_frequency_hz <- function(emg_signal, sampling_frequency_hz) {
  emg_centered <- emg_signal - mean(emg_signal) #recompute, last one is nested and global var is a dangerous world.
  n_samples <- length(emg_centered)
# Hann window to reduce spectral leakage 
  hann_window <- 0.5 - 0.5 * cos(2 * pi * (0:(n_samples - 1)) / (n_samples - 1))
  emg_windowed <- emg_centered * hann_window

# Single-sided power spectrum (FFT)
  fft_complex <- stats::fft(emg_windowed)
  n_half <- floor(n_samples / 2) + 1
  fft_half <- fft_complex[1:n_half]
  
# Power spectrum (magnitude squared of the FFT, normalized by the number of samples)
  power_spectrum <- (Mod(fft_half) ^ 2) / n_samples
  frequency_axis_hz <- (0:(n_half - 1)) * (sampling_frequency_hz / n_samples)
  
  #drop 0hz bin
  power_spectrum <- power_spectrum[-1]
frequency_axis_hz <- frequency_axis_hz[-1]

  # Cumulative power to find the median frequency
  cumulative_power <- cumsum(power_spectrum)
  median_index <- which(cumulative_power >= 0.5 * sum(power_spectrum))[1]
  
  frequency_axis_hz[median_index]
  }

5 Construction of Feature Tables

5.1 MVC Reference Features

channels <- c("emg6", "emg8", "emg10")

mvc_ref_split <- split(mvc_ref_mid1s_table, mvc_ref_mid1s_table$VC_count)

mvc_ref_mid1s_feature_table <- dplyr::bind_rows(lapply(names(mvc_ref_split), function(vc_id) {

  block <- mvc_ref_split[[vc_id]]

  torque_mean_Nm <- mean(block$torque_Nm, na.rm = TRUE)

  dplyr::bind_rows(lapply(channels, function(ch) {

    x <- block[[ch]]

    dplyr::tibble(
      SEQ = "MVC_REF",
      VC_count = as.integer(vc_id),
      channel = ch,
      rms_mid_1s = emg_rms(x),
      mdf_mid_1s_hz = emg_median_frequency_hz(x, emg_sampling_frequency_hz),
      torque_mean_Nm = torque_mean_Nm,
      n_samples = sum(is.finite(x))
    )
  }))
})) |>
  dplyr::arrange(channel, VC_count)

print(mvc_ref_mid1s_feature_table)
## # A tibble: 9 × 7
##   SEQ     VC_count channel rms_mid_1s mdf_mid_1s_hz torque_mean_Nm n_samples
##   <chr>      <int> <chr>        <dbl>         <dbl>          <dbl>     <int>
## 1 MVC_REF        1 emg10    0.000105           73.0           231.      2149
## 2 MVC_REF        2 emg10    0.000118           72.0           235.      2149
## 3 MVC_REF        3 emg10    0.000100           71.0           224.      2148
## 4 MVC_REF        1 emg6     0.000115           73.0           231.      2149
## 5 MVC_REF        2 emg6     0.000111           94.0           235.      2149
## 6 MVC_REF        3 emg6     0.000114           89.0           224.      2148
## 7 MVC_REF        1 emg8     0.0000807          74.0           231.      2149
## 8 MVC_REF        2 emg8     0.0000902          62.0           235.      2149
## 9 MVC_REF        3 emg8     0.0000742          66.0           224.      2148
channels <- c("emg6", "emg8", "emg10")

svc_ref_split <- split(svc_ref_mid1s_table, svc_ref_mid1s_table$VC_count)

svc_ref_mid1s_feature_table <- dplyr::bind_rows(lapply(names(svc_ref_split), function(vc_id) {

  block <- svc_ref_split[[vc_id]]
  torque_mean_Nm <- mean(block$torque_Nm, na.rm = TRUE)

  dplyr::bind_rows(lapply(channels, function(ch) {

    x <- block[[ch]]

    dplyr::tibble(
      SEQ = "SVC_REF",
      VC_count = as.integer(vc_id),
      channel = ch,
      rms_mid_1s = emg_rms(x),
      mdf_mid_1s_hz = emg_median_frequency_hz(x, emg_sampling_frequency_hz),
      torque_mean_Nm = torque_mean_Nm,
      n_samples = sum(is.finite(x))
    )
  }))
})) |>
  dplyr::arrange(channel, VC_count)

print(svc_ref_mid1s_feature_table)
## # A tibble: 9 × 7
##   SEQ     VC_count channel rms_mid_1s mdf_mid_1s_hz torque_mean_Nm n_samples
##   <chr>      <int> <chr>        <dbl>         <dbl>          <dbl>     <int>
## 1 SVC_REF        1 emg10    0.0000278          66.0           71.2      2148
## 2 SVC_REF        2 emg10    0.0000473          72.0          107.       2149
## 3 SVC_REF        3 emg10    0.0000699          68.0          153.       2149
## 4 SVC_REF        1 emg6     0.0000508          91.0           71.2      2148
## 5 SVC_REF        2 emg6     0.0000789          95.0          107.       2149
## 6 SVC_REF        3 emg6     0.0000844          89.0          153.       2149
## 7 SVC_REF        1 emg8     0.0000265          69.0           71.2      2148
## 8 SVC_REF        2 emg8     0.0000423          76.0          107.       2149
## 9 SVC_REF        3 emg8     0.0000540          73.0          153.       2149
svc_ref_mid1s_feature_table |> dplyr::count(channel) |> print()
## # A tibble: 3 × 2
##   channel     n
##   <chr>   <int>
## 1 emg10       3
## 2 emg6        3
## 3 emg8        3
mvc_recov_dyn_split <- split(mvc_recov_dyn_mid1s_table, mvc_recov_dyn_mid1s_table$VC_count)

mvc_recov_dyn_mid1s_feature_table <- dplyr::bind_rows(lapply(names(mvc_recov_dyn_split), function(vc_id) {

  block <- mvc_recov_dyn_split[[vc_id]]
  torque_mean_Nm <- mean(block$torque_Nm, na.rm = TRUE)

  dplyr::bind_rows(lapply(channels, function(ch) {

    x <- block[[ch]]

    dplyr::tibble(
      SEQ = "MVC_RECOV_DYN",
      VC_count = as.integer(vc_id),
      channel = ch,
      rms_mid_1s = emg_rms(x),
      mdf_mid_1s_hz = emg_median_frequency_hz(x, emg_sampling_frequency_hz),
      torque_mean_Nm = torque_mean_Nm,
      n_samples = sum(is.finite(x))
    )
  }))
})) |>
  dplyr::arrange(channel, VC_count)

print(mvc_recov_dyn_mid1s_feature_table)
## # A tibble: 3 × 7
##   SEQ         VC_count channel rms_mid_1s mdf_mid_1s_hz torque_mean_Nm n_samples
##   <chr>          <int> <chr>        <dbl>         <dbl>          <dbl>     <int>
## 1 MVC_RECOV_…        1 emg10    0.000142           72.0           221.      2148
## 2 MVC_RECOV_…        1 emg6     0.000143           78.0           221.      2148
## 3 MVC_RECOV_…        1 emg8     0.0000884          80.0           221.      2148
mvc_recov_sta_split <- split(mvc_recov_sta_mid1s_table, mvc_recov_sta_mid1s_table$VC_count)

mvc_recov_sta_mid1s_feature_table <- dplyr::bind_rows(lapply(names(mvc_recov_sta_split), function(vc_id) {

  block <- mvc_recov_sta_split[[vc_id]]
  torque_mean_Nm <- mean(block$torque_Nm, na.rm = TRUE)

  dplyr::bind_rows(lapply(channels, function(ch) {

    x <- block[[ch]]

    dplyr::tibble(
      SEQ = "MVC_RECOV_STA",
      VC_count = as.integer(vc_id),
      channel = ch,
      rms_mid_1s = emg_rms(x),
      mdf_mid_1s_hz = emg_median_frequency_hz(x, emg_sampling_frequency_hz),
      torque_mean_Nm = torque_mean_Nm,
      n_samples = sum(is.finite(x))
    )
  }))
})) |>
  dplyr::arrange(channel, VC_count)

print(mvc_recov_sta_mid1s_feature_table)
## # A tibble: 3 × 7
##   SEQ         VC_count channel rms_mid_1s mdf_mid_1s_hz torque_mean_Nm n_samples
##   <chr>          <int> <chr>        <dbl>         <dbl>          <dbl>     <int>
## 1 MVC_RECOV_…        1 emg10    0.000157           65.0           232.      2149
## 2 MVC_RECOV_…        1 emg6     0.000160           76.0           232.      2149
## 3 MVC_RECOV_…        1 emg8     0.0000976          82.0           232.      2149
reference_mid1s_feature_table <- dplyr::bind_rows(
  mvc_ref_mid1s_feature_table,
  svc_ref_mid1s_feature_table,
  mvc_recov_dyn_mid1s_feature_table,
  mvc_recov_sta_mid1s_feature_table
) |>
  dplyr::arrange(SEQ, channel, VC_count)

print(reference_mid1s_feature_table)
## # A tibble: 24 × 7
##    SEQ        VC_count channel rms_mid_1s mdf_mid_1s_hz torque_mean_Nm n_samples
##    <chr>         <int> <chr>        <dbl>         <dbl>          <dbl>     <int>
##  1 MVC_RECOV…        1 emg10    0.000142           72.0           221.      2148
##  2 MVC_RECOV…        1 emg6     0.000143           78.0           221.      2148
##  3 MVC_RECOV…        1 emg8     0.0000884          80.0           221.      2148
##  4 MVC_RECOV…        1 emg10    0.000157           65.0           232.      2149
##  5 MVC_RECOV…        1 emg6     0.000160           76.0           232.      2149
##  6 MVC_RECOV…        1 emg8     0.0000976          82.0           232.      2149
##  7 MVC_REF           1 emg10    0.000105           73.0           231.      2149
##  8 MVC_REF           2 emg10    0.000118           72.0           235.      2149
##  9 MVC_REF           3 emg10    0.000100           71.0           224.      2148
## 10 MVC_REF           1 emg6     0.000115           73.0           231.      2149
## # ℹ 14 more rows
mvc_level_summary <- reference_mid1s_feature_table |>
  dplyr::filter(SEQ %in% c("MVC_REF", "MVC_RECOV_DYN", "MVC_RECOV_STA")) |>
  dplyr::group_by(SEQ, channel) |>
  dplyr::summarise(
    n_reps = dplyr::n(),
    torque_mean_Nm = mean(torque_mean_Nm, na.rm = TRUE),
    rms_mid_1s_mean = mean(rms_mid_1s, na.rm = TRUE),
    mdf_mid_1s_hz_mean = mean(mdf_mid_1s_hz, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

mvc_level_summary
## # A tibble: 9 × 6
##   SEQ           channel n_reps torque_mean_Nm rms_mid_1s_mean mdf_mid_1s_hz_mean
##   <chr>         <chr>    <int>          <dbl>           <dbl>              <dbl>
## 1 MVC_RECOV_DYN emg10        1           221.       0.000142                72.0
## 2 MVC_RECOV_DYN emg6         1           221.       0.000143                78.0
## 3 MVC_RECOV_DYN emg8         1           221.       0.0000884               80.0
## 4 MVC_RECOV_STA emg10        1           232.       0.000157                65.0
## 5 MVC_RECOV_STA emg6         1           232.       0.000160                76.0
## 6 MVC_RECOV_STA emg8         1           232.       0.0000976               82.0
## 7 MVC_REF       emg10        3           230.       0.000108                72.0
## 8 MVC_REF       emg6         3           230.       0.000113                85.3
## 9 MVC_REF       emg8         3           230.       0.0000817               67.3
###mvc_ref_baseline <- mvc_level_summary |>
mvc_ref_baseline <- mvc_level_summary |>
  dplyr::filter(SEQ == "MVC_REF") |>
  dplyr::select(
    channel,
    torque_ref_Nm = torque_mean_Nm,
    rms_ref = rms_mid_1s_mean,
    mdf_ref_hz = mdf_mid_1s_hz_mean
  )

mvc_ref_baseline
## # A tibble: 3 × 4
##   channel torque_ref_Nm   rms_ref mdf_ref_hz
##   <chr>           <dbl>     <dbl>      <dbl>
## 1 emg10            230. 0.000108        72.0
## 2 emg6             230. 0.000113        85.3
## 3 emg8             230. 0.0000817       67.3
# Shared torque baseline for downstream normalized torque analyses
torque_mvc_ref_baseline <- mean(mvc_ref_baseline$torque_ref_Nm, na.rm = TRUE)

torque_mvc_ref_baseline
## [1] 230.0556

5.2 EX_DYN Feature Table

feature table per rep per channel

channels <- c("emg6", "emg8", "emg10")

ex_dyn_split <- split(ex_dyn_mid1s_table, ex_dyn_mid1s_table$VC_count)

ex_dyn_mid1s_feature_table <- dplyr::bind_rows(lapply(names(ex_dyn_split), function(vc_id) {

  block <- ex_dyn_split[[vc_id]]
  torque_mean_Nm <- mean(block$torque_Nm, na.rm = TRUE)

  dplyr::bind_rows(lapply(channels, function(ch) {

    x <- block[[ch]]

    dplyr::tibble(
      SEQ = "EX_DYN",
      VC_count = as.integer(vc_id),
      channel = ch,
      rms_mid_1s = emg_rms(x),
      mdf_mid_1s_hz = emg_median_frequency_hz(x, emg_sampling_frequency_hz),
      torque_mean_Nm = torque_mean_Nm,
      n_samples = sum(is.finite(x))
    )
  }))
})) |>
  dplyr::arrange(channel, VC_count)

5.3 EX_STA Feature Table

channels <- c("emg6", "emg8", "emg10")

ex_sta_split <- split(ex_sta_mid1s_table, ex_sta_mid1s_table$window_id)

ex_sta_mid1s_feature_table <- dplyr::bind_rows(lapply(names(ex_sta_split), function(win_id) {

  block <- ex_sta_split[[win_id]]
  torque_mean_Nm <- mean(block$torque_Nm, na.rm = TRUE)

  dplyr::bind_rows(lapply(channels, function(ch) {

    x <- block[[ch]]

    dplyr::tibble(
      SEQ = "EX_STA",
      window_id = as.integer(win_id),
      channel = ch,
      rms_mid_1s = emg_rms(x),
      mdf_mid_1s_hz = emg_median_frequency_hz(x, emg_sampling_frequency_hz),
      torque_mean_Nm = torque_mean_Nm,
      n_samples = sum(is.finite(x))
    )
  }))
})) |>
  dplyr::arrange(channel, window_id)

print(ex_sta_mid1s_feature_table)
## # A tibble: 180 × 7
##    SEQ    window_id channel rms_mid_1s mdf_mid_1s_hz torque_mean_Nm n_samples
##    <chr>      <int> <chr>        <dbl>         <dbl>          <dbl>     <int>
##  1 EX_STA         1 emg10    0.0000682          64.0           89.0      2149
##  2 EX_STA         2 emg10    0.000110           61.0          142.       2148
##  3 EX_STA         3 emg10    0.000116           73.0          151.       2148
##  4 EX_STA         4 emg10    0.000131           58.0          161.       2148
##  5 EX_STA         5 emg10    0.000148           58.0          164.       2148
##  6 EX_STA         6 emg10    0.000140           62.0          161.       2148
##  7 EX_STA         7 emg10    0.000128           62.0          156.       2149
##  8 EX_STA         8 emg10    0.000126           65.0          154.       2148
##  9 EX_STA         9 emg10    0.000120           61.0          157.       2148
## 10 EX_STA        10 emg10    0.000133           65.0          160.       2148
## # ℹ 170 more rows

6 Baseline Normalization

6.1 MVC Baseline Computation

mvc_ref_mid1s_rms_baseline_table <- mvc_ref_mid1s_feature_table |>
  dplyr::group_by(channel) |>
  dplyr::summarise(
    rms_mvc_ref_mid1s_baseline = mean(rms_mid_1s, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) |>
  dplyr::arrange(channel)

print(mvc_ref_mid1s_rms_baseline_table)
## # A tibble: 3 × 2
##   channel rms_mvc_ref_mid1s_baseline
##   <chr>                        <dbl>
## 1 emg10                    0.000108 
## 2 emg6                     0.000113 
## 3 emg8                     0.0000817
# Extend the existing 30/50/70 reference set with a 100% point from MVC_REF.
# The 100% row is an aggregated MVC_REF summary, not a true fourth SVC repetition.
# VC_count = 4L is kept only as a compatibility placeholder; plot/order logic should use reference_level_id.
reference_level_feature_table <- dplyr::bind_rows(
  svc_ref_mid1s_feature_table |>
    dplyr::mutate(
      reference_level_id = VC_count,
      reference_level = factor(
        VC_count,
        levels = c(1, 2, 3, 4),
        labels = c("30%", "50%", "70%", "100%")
      )
    ),
  mvc_ref_mid1s_feature_table |>
    dplyr::group_by(channel) |>
    dplyr::summarise(
      SEQ = "MVC_REF",
      VC_count = 4L,
      rms_mid_1s = mean(rms_mid_1s, na.rm = TRUE),
      mdf_mid_1s_hz = mean(mdf_mid_1s_hz, na.rm = TRUE),
      torque_mean_Nm = mean(torque_mean_Nm, na.rm = TRUE),
      n_samples = mean(n_samples, na.rm = TRUE),
      .groups = "drop"
    ) |>
    dplyr::mutate(
      reference_level_id = 4L,
      reference_level = factor(
        reference_level_id,
        levels = c(1, 2, 3, 4),
        labels = c("30%", "50%", "70%", "100%")
      )
    )
) |>
  dplyr::arrange(channel, reference_level_id)

print(reference_level_feature_table)
## # A tibble: 12 × 9
##    SEQ     VC_count channel rms_mid_1s mdf_mid_1s_hz torque_mean_Nm n_samples
##    <chr>      <int> <chr>        <dbl>         <dbl>          <dbl>     <dbl>
##  1 SVC_REF        1 emg10    0.0000278          66.0           71.2     2148 
##  2 SVC_REF        2 emg10    0.0000473          72.0          107.      2149 
##  3 SVC_REF        3 emg10    0.0000699          68.0          153.      2149 
##  4 MVC_REF        4 emg10    0.000108           72.0          230.      2149.
##  5 SVC_REF        1 emg6     0.0000508          91.0           71.2     2148 
##  6 SVC_REF        2 emg6     0.0000789          95.0          107.      2149 
##  7 SVC_REF        3 emg6     0.0000844          89.0          153.      2149 
##  8 MVC_REF        4 emg6     0.000113           85.3          230.      2149.
##  9 SVC_REF        1 emg8     0.0000265          69.0           71.2     2148 
## 10 SVC_REF        2 emg8     0.0000423          76.0          107.      2149 
## 11 SVC_REF        3 emg8     0.0000540          73.0          153.      2149 
## 12 MVC_REF        4 emg8     0.0000817          67.3          230.      2149.
## # ℹ 2 more variables: reference_level_id <int>, reference_level <fct>
# Build a compact per-channel calibration fit from the unified 30/50/70/100 reference set.
# The fitted relationship maps calibrated torque to the expected channel-specific reference EMG feature.
reference_calibration_input_table <- reference_level_feature_table |>
  dplyr::select(
    channel,
    reference_level_id,
    reference_level,
    torque_mean_Nm,
    rms_mid_1s,
    mdf_mid_1s_hz
  ) |>
  tidyr::pivot_longer(
    cols = c(rms_mid_1s, mdf_mid_1s_hz),
    names_to = "response_variable",
    values_to = "response_value"
  ) |>
  dplyr::mutate(
    fit_type = "linear",
    predictor_variable = "torque_mean_Nm",
    predictor_definition = "Mean calibrated torque (Nm) from the middle 1 s reference window",
    response_definition = dplyr::case_when(
      response_variable == "rms_mid_1s" ~ "RMS computed on the middle 1 s reference window",
      response_variable == "mdf_mid_1s_hz" ~ "Median frequency (Hz) computed on the middle 1 s reference window",
      TRUE ~ NA_character_
    )
  ) |>
  dplyr::filter(
    is.finite(torque_mean_Nm),
    is.finite(response_value)
  ) |>
  dplyr::arrange(channel, response_variable, reference_level_id)

reference_calibration_table <- reference_calibration_input_table |>
  dplyr::group_by(
    channel,
    response_variable,
    fit_type,
    predictor_variable,
    predictor_definition,
    response_definition
  ) |>
  dplyr::summarise(
    n_reference_levels = dplyr::n(),
    slope = unname(coef(lm(response_value ~ torque_mean_Nm))[2]),
    intercept = unname(coef(lm(response_value ~ torque_mean_Nm))[1]),
    r_squared = summary(lm(response_value ~ torque_mean_Nm))$r.squared,
    .groups = "drop"
  ) |>
  dplyr::arrange(response_variable, channel)

print(reference_calibration_table)
## # A tibble: 6 × 10
##   channel response_variable fit_type predictor_variable predictor_definition    
##   <chr>   <chr>             <chr>    <chr>              <chr>                   
## 1 emg10   mdf_mid_1s_hz     linear   torque_mean_Nm     Mean calibrated torque …
## 2 emg6    mdf_mid_1s_hz     linear   torque_mean_Nm     Mean calibrated torque …
## 3 emg8    mdf_mid_1s_hz     linear   torque_mean_Nm     Mean calibrated torque …
## 4 emg10   rms_mid_1s        linear   torque_mean_Nm     Mean calibrated torque …
## 5 emg6    rms_mid_1s        linear   torque_mean_Nm     Mean calibrated torque …
## 6 emg8    rms_mid_1s        linear   torque_mean_Nm     Mean calibrated torque …
## # ℹ 5 more variables: response_definition <chr>, n_reference_levels <int>,
## #   slope <dbl>, intercept <dbl>, r_squared <dbl>
# Compare observed EX_DYN RMS against the validated per-channel reference fit in torque_mean_Nm space.
ex_dyn_reference_rms_fit_table <- reference_calibration_table |>
  dplyr::filter(response_variable == "rms_mid_1s") |>
  dplyr::select(
    channel,
    response_variable,
    predictor_variable,
    fit_type,
    slope,
    intercept,
    r_squared
  )

ex_dyn_reference_rms_comparison_table <- ex_dyn_mid1s_feature_table |>
  dplyr::select(
    SEQ,
    VC_count,
    channel,
    torque_mean_Nm,
    rms_mid_1s
  ) |>
  dplyr::left_join(
    ex_dyn_reference_rms_fit_table,
    by = "channel"
  ) |>
  dplyr::mutate(
    observed_rms_mid_1s = rms_mid_1s,
    expected_rms_mid_1s = intercept + slope * torque_mean_Nm,
    residual_rms_mid_1s = observed_rms_mid_1s - expected_rms_mid_1s,
    relative_residual_rms = dplyr::if_else(
      is.finite(expected_rms_mid_1s) & expected_rms_mid_1s != 0,
      residual_rms_mid_1s / expected_rms_mid_1s,
      NA_real_
    )
  ) |>
  dplyr::select(
    SEQ,
    VC_count,
    channel,
    torque_mean_Nm,
    response_variable,
    predictor_variable,
    fit_type,
    observed_rms_mid_1s,
    expected_rms_mid_1s,
    residual_rms_mid_1s,
    relative_residual_rms,
    r_squared
  ) |>
  dplyr::arrange(channel, VC_count)

print(ex_dyn_reference_rms_comparison_table)
## # A tibble: 186 × 12
##    SEQ    VC_count channel torque_mean_Nm response_variable predictor_variable
##    <chr>     <int> <chr>            <dbl> <chr>             <chr>             
##  1 EX_DYN        1 emg10             237. rms_mid_1s        torque_mean_Nm    
##  2 EX_DYN        2 emg10             254. rms_mid_1s        torque_mean_Nm    
##  3 EX_DYN        3 emg10             237. rms_mid_1s        torque_mean_Nm    
##  4 EX_DYN        4 emg10             237. rms_mid_1s        torque_mean_Nm    
##  5 EX_DYN        5 emg10             233. rms_mid_1s        torque_mean_Nm    
##  6 EX_DYN        6 emg10             230. rms_mid_1s        torque_mean_Nm    
##  7 EX_DYN        7 emg10             219. rms_mid_1s        torque_mean_Nm    
##  8 EX_DYN        8 emg10             208. rms_mid_1s        torque_mean_Nm    
##  9 EX_DYN        9 emg10             209. rms_mid_1s        torque_mean_Nm    
## 10 EX_DYN       10 emg10             209. rms_mid_1s        torque_mean_Nm    
## # ℹ 176 more rows
## # ℹ 6 more variables: fit_type <chr>, observed_rms_mid_1s <dbl>,
## #   expected_rms_mid_1s <dbl>, residual_rms_mid_1s <dbl>,
## #   relative_residual_rms <dbl>, r_squared <dbl>
reference_rms_points_plot_df <- reference_level_feature_table |>
  dplyr::select(channel, reference_level, torque_mean_Nm, rms_mid_1s)

ex_dyn_reference_rms_overlay_plot <- ggplot() +
  geom_abline(
    data = ex_dyn_reference_rms_fit_table,
    aes(intercept = intercept, slope = slope, color = "Reference fit"),
    linewidth = 0.9,
    alpha = 0.9,
    show.legend = TRUE
  ) +
  geom_point(
    data = reference_rms_points_plot_df,
    aes(x = torque_mean_Nm, y = rms_mid_1s, color = "Reference levels"),
    size = 2.1,
    alpha = 0.9
  ) +
  geom_point(
    data = ex_dyn_reference_rms_comparison_table,
    aes(x = torque_mean_Nm, y = observed_rms_mid_1s, color = "Observed EX_DYN"),
    size = 1.7,
    alpha = 0.8
  ) +
  facet_wrap(~ channel, scales = "free_y") +
  scale_color_manual(
    values = c(
      "Reference fit" = "#4d4d4d",
      "Reference levels" = "#a6a6a6",
      "Observed EX_DYN" = "#1b6ca8"
    ),
    breaks = c("Reference fit", "Reference levels", "Observed EX_DYN")
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "EX_DYN observed RMS against subject-specific reference fit",
    subtitle = "Background: validated four-level reference relationship in torque_mean_Nm space",
    x = "Torque mean (Nm)",
    y = "RMS (a.u.)",
    color = NULL
  ) +
  theme(legend.position = "top")

print(ex_dyn_reference_rms_overlay_plot)

mvc_torque_plot_df <- mvc_level_summary |>
  dplyr::distinct(SEQ, torque_mean_Nm) |>
  dplyr::mutate(
    SEQ = factor(SEQ, levels = c("MVC_REF", "MVC_RECOV_DYN", "MVC_RECOV_STA"))
  )

ggplot(mvc_torque_plot_df, aes(x = SEQ, y = torque_mean_Nm)) +
  geom_point(size = 3) +
  geom_line(aes(group = 1)) +
  labs(
    title = "MVC — Torque baseline vs recovery",
    x = NULL,
    y = "Torque (Nm)"
  )

mvc_rms_plot_df <- mvc_level_summary |>
  dplyr::mutate(
    SEQ = factor(SEQ, levels = c("MVC_REF", "MVC_RECOV_DYN", "MVC_RECOV_STA"))
  )

ggplot(mvc_rms_plot_df, aes(x = SEQ, y = rms_mid_1s_mean)) +
  geom_point(size = 2) +
  geom_line(aes(group = channel)) +
  facet_wrap(~ channel, scales = "free_y") +
  labs(
    title = "MVC — RMS baseline vs recovery (middle 1s)",
    x = NULL,
    y = "RMS (a.u.)"
  )

mvc_mdf_plot_df <- mvc_level_summary |>
  dplyr::mutate(
    SEQ = factor(SEQ, levels = c("MVC_REF", "MVC_RECOV_DYN", "MVC_RECOV_STA"))
  )

ggplot(mvc_mdf_plot_df, aes(x = SEQ, y = mdf_mid_1s_hz_mean)) +
  geom_point(size = 2) +
  geom_line(aes(group = channel)) +
  facet_wrap(~ channel, scales = "free_y") +
  labs(
    title = "MVC — MDF baseline vs recovery (middle 1s)",
    x = NULL,
    y = "MDF (Hz)"
  )

#turn it dynamic later, if we keep ?
ex_dyn_peak <- ex_dyn_mid1s_feature_table |>
  dplyr::slice_max(torque_mean_Nm, n = 3) |>
  dplyr::mutate(SEQ = "EX_DYN_PEAK")

ex_dyn_peak
## # A tibble: 3 × 7
##   SEQ         VC_count channel rms_mid_1s mdf_mid_1s_hz torque_mean_Nm n_samples
##   <chr>          <int> <chr>        <dbl>         <dbl>          <dbl>     <int>
## 1 EX_DYN_PEAK        2 emg10    0.000128           72.0           254.      2148
## 2 EX_DYN_PEAK        2 emg6     0.000146          102.            254.      2148
## 3 EX_DYN_PEAK        2 emg8     0.0000953          79.0           254.      2148
# mvc_dyn_recovery_table}

mvc_dyn_recovery_df <- dplyr::bind_rows(
  mvc_level_summary,
  ex_dyn_peak
) |>
  dplyr::filter(
    SEQ %in% c("MVC_REF", "EX_DYN_PEAK", "MVC_RECOV_DYN", "MVC_RECOV_STA")
  )

mvc_dyn_recovery_df
## # A tibble: 12 × 10
##    SEQ          channel n_reps torque_mean_Nm rms_mid_1s_mean mdf_mid_1s_hz_mean
##    <chr>        <chr>    <int>          <dbl>           <dbl>              <dbl>
##  1 MVC_RECOV_D… emg10        1           221.       0.000142                72.0
##  2 MVC_RECOV_D… emg6         1           221.       0.000143                78.0
##  3 MVC_RECOV_D… emg8         1           221.       0.0000884               80.0
##  4 MVC_RECOV_S… emg10        1           232.       0.000157                65.0
##  5 MVC_RECOV_S… emg6         1           232.       0.000160                76.0
##  6 MVC_RECOV_S… emg8         1           232.       0.0000976               82.0
##  7 MVC_REF      emg10        3           230.       0.000108                72.0
##  8 MVC_REF      emg6         3           230.       0.000113                85.3
##  9 MVC_REF      emg8         3           230.       0.0000817               67.3
## 10 EX_DYN_PEAK  emg10       NA           254.      NA                       NA  
## 11 EX_DYN_PEAK  emg6        NA           254.      NA                       NA  
## 12 EX_DYN_PEAK  emg8        NA           254.      NA                       NA  
## # ℹ 4 more variables: VC_count <int>, rms_mid_1s <dbl>, mdf_mid_1s_hz <dbl>,
## #   n_samples <int>
# ex_dynpeak (not dynamic yet)
ex_dyn_peak_h <- ex_dyn_mid1s_feature_table |>
  dplyr::slice_max(torque_mean_Nm, n = 3) |>
  dplyr::mutate(
    SEQ = "EX_DYN_PEAK",
    n_reps = 1L,
    rms_mid_1s_mean = rms_mid_1s,
    mdf_mid_1s_hz_mean = mdf_mid_1s_hz
  ) |>
  dplyr::select(
    SEQ, channel, n_reps,
    torque_mean_Nm,
    rms_mid_1s_mean,
    mdf_mid_1s_hz_mean
  )

# traj
mvc_dyn_recovery_df <- dplyr::bind_rows(
  mvc_level_summary |>
    dplyr::select(SEQ, channel, n_reps, torque_mean_Nm, rms_mid_1s_mean, mdf_mid_1s_hz_mean),
  ex_dyn_peak_h
) |>
  dplyr::filter(SEQ %in% c("MVC_REF", "EX_DYN_PEAK", "MVC_RECOV_DYN", "MVC_RECOV_STA")) |>
  dplyr::mutate(
    SEQ = factor(SEQ, levels = c("MVC_REF", "EX_DYN_PEAK", "MVC_RECOV_DYN", "MVC_RECOV_STA"))
  )

# build table
plot_long <- mvc_dyn_recovery_df |>
  dplyr::group_by(SEQ) |>
  dplyr::mutate(torque_mean_Nm = mean(torque_mean_Nm)) |>
  dplyr::ungroup() |>
  tidyr::pivot_longer(
    cols = c(torque_mean_Nm, rms_mid_1s_mean, mdf_mid_1s_hz_mean),
    names_to = "metric",
    values_to = "value"
  ) |>
  dplyr::mutate(
    metric = dplyr::recode(
      metric,
      torque_mean_Nm = "Torque (Nm)",
      rms_mid_1s_mean = "RMS (a.u.)",
      mdf_mid_1s_hz_mean = "MDF (Hz)"
    ),
    metric = factor(metric, levels = c("Torque (Nm)", "RMS (a.u.)", "MDF (Hz)"))
  ) |>
  dplyr::filter(is.finite(value))

# Plot
ggplot(plot_long, aes(x = SEQ, y = value)) +
  geom_point(size = 2) +
  geom_line(aes(group = channel)) +
  facet_grid(metric ~ channel, scales = "free_y") +
  scale_x_discrete(
    labels = c(
      MVC_REF = "MVC",
      EX_DYN_PEAK = "EX_DYN peak",
      MVC_RECOV_DYN = "RECOV dyn",
      MVC_RECOV_STA = "RECOV sta"
    )
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "REF -> DYN -> R_DYN -> R_STA",
    x = NULL,
    y = NULL
  ) +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 30, hjust = 1))

check (emg 10 ??)

# table
mvc_dyn_recovery_check_wide <- mvc_dyn_recovery_df |>
  dplyr::mutate(
    SEQ = factor(SEQ, levels = c("MVC_REF", "EX_DYN_PEAK", "MVC_RECOV_DYN", "MVC_RECOV_STA"))
  ) |>
  dplyr::arrange(channel, SEQ) |>
  dplyr::select(SEQ, channel, torque_mean_Nm, rms_mid_1s_mean, mdf_mid_1s_hz_mean) |>
  tidyr::pivot_wider(
    names_from = SEQ,
    values_from = c(torque_mean_Nm, rms_mid_1s_mean, mdf_mid_1s_hz_mean),
    names_glue = "{.value}__{SEQ}"
  )

mvc_dyn_recovery_check_wide
## # A tibble: 3 × 13
##   channel torque_mean_Nm__MVC_REF torque_mean_Nm__EX_DY…¹ torque_mean_Nm__MVC_…²
##   <chr>                     <dbl>                   <dbl>                  <dbl>
## 1 emg10                      230.                    254.                   221.
## 2 emg6                       230.                    254.                   221.
## 3 emg8                       230.                    254.                   221.
## # ℹ abbreviated names: ¹​torque_mean_Nm__EX_DYN_PEAK,
## #   ²​torque_mean_Nm__MVC_RECOV_DYN
## # ℹ 9 more variables: torque_mean_Nm__MVC_RECOV_STA <dbl>,
## #   rms_mid_1s_mean__MVC_REF <dbl>, rms_mid_1s_mean__EX_DYN_PEAK <dbl>,
## #   rms_mid_1s_mean__MVC_RECOV_DYN <dbl>, rms_mid_1s_mean__MVC_RECOV_STA <dbl>,
## #   mdf_mid_1s_hz_mean__MVC_REF <dbl>, mdf_mid_1s_hz_mean__EX_DYN_PEAK <dbl>,
## #   mdf_mid_1s_hz_mean__MVC_RECOV_DYN <dbl>, …

6.2 RMS Normalization

normalize ex_dyn

ex_dyn_mid1s_feature_table_norm <- ex_dyn_mid1s_feature_table |>
  dplyr::left_join(mvc_ref_mid1s_rms_baseline_table, by = "channel") |>
  dplyr::mutate(
    rms_norm = rms_mid_1s / rms_mvc_ref_mid1s_baseline,
    torque_norm = torque_mean_Nm / torque_mvc_ref_baseline
  )

print(ex_dyn_mid1s_feature_table_norm)
## # A tibble: 186 × 10
##    SEQ    VC_count channel rms_mid_1s mdf_mid_1s_hz torque_mean_Nm n_samples
##    <chr>     <int> <chr>        <dbl>         <dbl>          <dbl>     <int>
##  1 EX_DYN        1 emg10     0.000121          68.0           237.      2149
##  2 EX_DYN        2 emg10     0.000128          72.0           254.      2148
##  3 EX_DYN        3 emg10     0.000142          60.0           237.      2148
##  4 EX_DYN        4 emg10     0.000132          75.0           237.      2148
##  5 EX_DYN        5 emg10     0.000129          68.0           233.      2148
##  6 EX_DYN        6 emg10     0.000116          67.0           230.      2148
##  7 EX_DYN        7 emg10     0.000125          71.0           219.      2149
##  8 EX_DYN        8 emg10     0.000113          70.0           208.      2149
##  9 EX_DYN        9 emg10     0.000134          66.0           209.      2148
## 10 EX_DYN       10 emg10     0.000120          66.0           209.      2148
## # ℹ 176 more rows
## # ℹ 3 more variables: rms_mvc_ref_mid1s_baseline <dbl>, rms_norm <dbl>,
## #   torque_norm <dbl>

normalize ex_sta

ex_sta_mid1s_feature_table_norm <- ex_sta_mid1s_feature_table |>
  dplyr::left_join(mvc_ref_mid1s_rms_baseline_table, by = "channel") |>
  dplyr::mutate(
    rms_norm = rms_mid_1s / rms_mvc_ref_mid1s_baseline,
    torque_norm = torque_mean_Nm / torque_mvc_ref_baseline
  )

print(ex_sta_mid1s_feature_table_norm)
## # A tibble: 180 × 10
##    SEQ    window_id channel rms_mid_1s mdf_mid_1s_hz torque_mean_Nm n_samples
##    <chr>      <int> <chr>        <dbl>         <dbl>          <dbl>     <int>
##  1 EX_STA         1 emg10    0.0000682          64.0           89.0      2149
##  2 EX_STA         2 emg10    0.000110           61.0          142.       2148
##  3 EX_STA         3 emg10    0.000116           73.0          151.       2148
##  4 EX_STA         4 emg10    0.000131           58.0          161.       2148
##  5 EX_STA         5 emg10    0.000148           58.0          164.       2148
##  6 EX_STA         6 emg10    0.000140           62.0          161.       2148
##  7 EX_STA         7 emg10    0.000128           62.0          156.       2149
##  8 EX_STA         8 emg10    0.000126           65.0          154.       2148
##  9 EX_STA         9 emg10    0.000120           61.0          157.       2148
## 10 EX_STA        10 emg10    0.000133           65.0          160.       2148
## # ℹ 170 more rows
## # ℹ 3 more variables: rms_mvc_ref_mid1s_baseline <dbl>, rms_norm <dbl>,
## #   torque_norm <dbl>
# Fatigue-oriented ratio: normalized EMG demand per unit normalized torque output
ex_dyn_emg_torque_ratio_table <- ex_dyn_mid1s_feature_table_norm |>
  dplyr::mutate(
    emg_torque_ratio = rms_norm / torque_norm
  ) |>
  dplyr::select(
    SEQ, VC_count, channel,
    rms_norm, torque_norm, emg_torque_ratio
  )

ex_sta_emg_torque_ratio_table <- ex_sta_mid1s_feature_table_norm |>
  dplyr::left_join(
    ex_sta_window_table |>
      dplyr::select(window_id, window_mid_s),
    by = "window_id"
  ) |>
  dplyr::mutate(
    emg_torque_ratio = rms_norm / torque_norm
  ) |>
  dplyr::select(
    SEQ, window_id, window_mid_s, channel,
    rms_norm, torque_norm, emg_torque_ratio
  )

print(ex_dyn_emg_torque_ratio_table)
## # A tibble: 186 × 6
##    SEQ    VC_count channel rms_norm torque_norm emg_torque_ratio
##    <chr>     <int> <chr>      <dbl>       <dbl>            <dbl>
##  1 EX_DYN        1 emg10       1.13       1.03              1.09
##  2 EX_DYN        2 emg10       1.19       1.10              1.08
##  3 EX_DYN        3 emg10       1.31       1.03              1.28
##  4 EX_DYN        4 emg10       1.22       1.03              1.19
##  5 EX_DYN        5 emg10       1.19       1.01              1.18
##  6 EX_DYN        6 emg10       1.08       0.999             1.08
##  7 EX_DYN        7 emg10       1.16       0.953             1.22
##  8 EX_DYN        8 emg10       1.04       0.905             1.15
##  9 EX_DYN        9 emg10       1.24       0.907             1.36
## 10 EX_DYN       10 emg10       1.11       0.910             1.22
## # ℹ 176 more rows
print(ex_sta_emg_torque_ratio_table)
## # A tibble: 180 × 7
##    SEQ    window_id window_mid_s channel rms_norm torque_norm emg_torque_ratio
##    <chr>      <int>        <dbl> <chr>      <dbl>       <dbl>            <dbl>
##  1 EX_STA         1        2067. emg10      0.632       0.387             1.63
##  2 EX_STA         2        2068. emg10      1.02        0.619             1.65
##  3 EX_STA         3        2069. emg10      1.08        0.656             1.64
##  4 EX_STA         4        2070. emg10      1.21        0.700             1.73
##  5 EX_STA         5        2071. emg10      1.37        0.715             1.92
##  6 EX_STA         6        2072. emg10      1.29        0.701             1.85
##  7 EX_STA         7        2073. emg10      1.18        0.677             1.75
##  8 EX_STA         8        2074. emg10      1.16        0.671             1.73
##  9 EX_STA         9        2075. emg10      1.11        0.683             1.63
## 10 EX_STA        10        2076. emg10      1.23        0.694             1.77
## # ℹ 170 more rows
# Explicit per-channel linear slopes for the existing fatigue ratio tables.
ex_dyn_emg_torque_ratio_slope_summary_table <- ex_dyn_emg_torque_ratio_table |>
  dplyr::group_by(SEQ, channel) |>
  dplyr::summarise(
    time_index_variable = "VC_count",
    fit_type = "lm(emg_torque_ratio ~ VC_count)",
    slope_estimate = unname(coef(lm(emg_torque_ratio ~ VC_count))[2]),
    intercept = unname(coef(lm(emg_torque_ratio ~ VC_count))[1]),
    r_squared = summary(lm(emg_torque_ratio ~ VC_count))$r.squared,
    n_points = dplyr::n(),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) |>
  dplyr::arrange(channel)

ex_sta_emg_torque_ratio_slope_summary_table <- ex_sta_emg_torque_ratio_table |>
  dplyr::group_by(SEQ, channel) |>
  dplyr::summarise(
    time_index_variable = "window_mid_s",
    fit_type = "lm(emg_torque_ratio ~ window_mid_s)",
    slope_estimate = unname(coef(lm(emg_torque_ratio ~ window_mid_s))[2]),
    intercept = unname(coef(lm(emg_torque_ratio ~ window_mid_s))[1]),
    r_squared = summary(lm(emg_torque_ratio ~ window_mid_s))$r.squared,
    n_points = dplyr::n(),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) |>
  dplyr::arrange(channel)

emg_torque_ratio_slope_summary_table <- dplyr::bind_rows(
  ex_dyn_emg_torque_ratio_slope_summary_table,
  ex_sta_emg_torque_ratio_slope_summary_table
) |>
  dplyr::select(
    SEQ,
    channel,
    time_index_variable,
    slope_estimate,
    intercept,
    r_squared,
    n_points,
    fit_type
  ) |>
  dplyr::arrange(SEQ, channel)

print(emg_torque_ratio_slope_summary_table)
## # A tibble: 6 × 8
##   SEQ    channel time_index_variable slope_estimate intercept r_squared n_points
##   <chr>  <chr>   <chr>                        <dbl>     <dbl>     <dbl>    <int>
## 1 EX_DYN emg10   VC_count                   0.0177       1.10     0.613       62
## 2 EX_DYN emg6    VC_count                   0.00976      1.11     0.478       62
## 3 EX_DYN emg8    VC_count                   0.0219       1.08     0.763       62
## 4 EX_STA emg10   window_mid_s               0.00770    -14.2      0.248       60
## 5 EX_STA emg6    window_mid_s              -0.00639     15.1      0.317       60
## 6 EX_STA emg8    window_mid_s               0.0110     -21.5      0.670       60
## # ℹ 1 more variable: fit_type <chr>

7 Fatigue Trend Modeling

7.1 MVC/SVC Trends

svc_df <- reference_level_feature_table |>
  dplyr::mutate(
    reference_level_id = as.integer(reference_level_id),
    intensity = factor(reference_level,
                       levels = c("30%", "50%", "70%", "100%")),
    torque_Nm = as.numeric(torque_mean_Nm)
  ) |>
  dplyr::filter(
    is.finite(reference_level_id),
    is.finite(torque_Nm),
    is.finite(rms_mid_1s),
    is.finite(mdf_mid_1s_hz)
  )

p_mdf <- ggplot(svc_df, aes(x = intensity, y = mdf_mid_1s_hz, group = 1)) +
  geom_point(size = 2) +
  geom_line() +
  facet_wrap(~ channel, scales = "free_y") +
  labs(
    title = "Reference set - MDF (middle 1s)",
    x = "Reference level (% MVC)",
    y = "MDF (Hz)"
  )

p_mdf

p_rms <- ggplot(svc_df, aes(x = intensity, y = rms_mid_1s, group = 1)) +
  geom_point(size = 2) +
  geom_line() +
  facet_wrap(~ channel, scales = "free_y") +
  labs(
    title = "Reference set - RMS across intensities (middle 1s)",
    x = "Reference level (% MVC)",
    y = "RMS (a.u.)"
  )

p_rms

# %EMG and %Force ()
svc_pct_df <- reference_level_feature_table |>
  dplyr::left_join(mvc_ref_mid1s_rms_baseline_table, by = "channel") |>
  dplyr::mutate(
    reference_level_id = as.integer(reference_level_id),
    torque_pct = 100 * torque_mean_Nm / torque_mvc_ref_baseline,
    emg_pct    = 100 * rms_mid_1s / rms_mvc_ref_mid1s_baseline,
    intensity  = factor(reference_level, levels = c("30%", "50%", "70%", "100%"))
  ) |>
  dplyr::filter(is.finite(reference_level_id))

# %EMG vs %Force 
ggplot(svc_pct_df, aes(x = torque_pct, y = emg_pct)) +
  geom_point(aes(shape = intensity), size = 2) +
  geom_line(aes(group = 1)) +
  facet_wrap(~ channel, scales = "free_y") +
  labs(
    title = "Reference set - %EMG vs %Force (normalized to MVC_REF)",
    x = "% Force (Torque / MVC_REF torque)",
    y = "% EMG (RMS / MVC_REF RMS)"
  )

svc_pct_summary_table <- svc_pct_df |>
  dplyr::group_by(channel) |>
  dplyr::summarise(
    `Slope (%EMG / %Force)` = coef(lm(emg_pct ~ torque_pct))[2],
    `Pearson r (emg_pct, torque_pct)` = cor(emg_pct, torque_pct, use = "complete.obs"),
    `R² (emg_pct ~ torque_pct)` = summary(lm(emg_pct ~ torque_pct))$r.squared,
    .groups = "drop"
  )

svc_pct_summary_table
## # A tibble: 3 × 4
##   channel `Slope (%EMG / %Force)` Pearson r (emg_pct, t…¹ R² (emg_pct ~ torque…²
##   <chr>                     <dbl>                   <dbl>                  <dbl>
## 1 emg10                     1.07                    1.000                  1.000
## 2 emg6                      0.734                   0.968                  0.937
## 3 emg8                      0.953                   0.997                  0.994
## # ℹ abbreviated names: ¹​`Pearson r (emg_pct, torque_pct)`,
## #   ²​`R² (emg_pct ~ torque_pct)`

7.2 EX_DYN Trends

ggplot(ex_dyn_mid1s_feature_table_norm,
       aes(x = VC_count, y = torque_norm)) +
  geom_point() +
  geom_line() +
  facet_wrap(~ channel) +
  labs(
    title = "EX_DYN - Normalized torque across repetitions",
    x = "Repetition (VC_count)",
    y = "Normalized torque (MVC_REF = 1)"
  )

ggplot(ex_dyn_mid1s_feature_table_norm,
       aes(x = torque_norm, y = rms_norm)) +
  geom_point() +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm") +
  facet_wrap(~ channel) +
  labs(
    title = "EX_DYN - EMG activation vs normalized torque",
    x = "Normalized torque (MVC_REF = 1)",
    y = "Normalized RMS"
  )
## `geom_smooth()` using formula = 'y ~ x'

ggplot(ex_dyn_mid1s_feature_table_norm,
       aes(x = VC_count, y = mdf_mid_1s_hz)) +
  geom_point() +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm") +
  facet_wrap(~ channel) +
  labs(
    title = "EX_DYN - Median frequency across repetitions",
    x = "Repetition (VC_count)",
    y = "Median frequency (Hz)"
  )
## `geom_smooth()` using formula = 'y ~ x'

ggplot(ex_dyn_mid1s_feature_table_norm,
       aes(x = torque_norm, y = mdf_mid_1s_hz)) +
  geom_point() +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm") +
  facet_wrap(~ channel) +
  labs(
    title = "EX_DYN - Median frequency vs normalized torque",
    x = "Normalized torque (MVC_REF = 1)",
    y = "Median frequency (Hz)"
  )
## `geom_smooth()` using formula = 'y ~ x'

ex_dyn_dual_axis_params <- ex_dyn_mid1s_feature_table_norm |>
  dplyr::group_by(channel) |>
  dplyr::summarise(
    rms_min = min(rms_norm, na.rm = TRUE),
    rms_max = max(rms_norm, na.rm = TRUE),
    mdf_min = min(mdf_mid_1s_hz, na.rm = TRUE),
    mdf_max = max(mdf_mid_1s_hz, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) |>
  dplyr::mutate(
    rms_span = rms_max - rms_min,
    mdf_span = mdf_max - mdf_min,
    scale_factor = dplyr::if_else(rms_span > 0 & mdf_span > 0, rms_span / mdf_span, 1)
  )

ex_dyn_dual_axis_plot_data <- ex_dyn_mid1s_feature_table_norm |>
  dplyr::left_join(ex_dyn_dual_axis_params, by = "channel") |>
  dplyr::mutate(
    mdf_scaled = (mdf_mid_1s_hz - mdf_min) * scale_factor + rms_min
  )

ex_dyn_dual_axis_plots <- lapply(unique(ex_dyn_dual_axis_plot_data$channel), function(ch) {
  plot_df <- ex_dyn_dual_axis_plot_data |>
    dplyr::filter(channel == ch)

  rms_min <- plot_df$rms_min[1]
  rms_max <- plot_df$rms_max[1]
  mdf_min <- plot_df$mdf_min[1]
  scale_factor <- plot_df$scale_factor[1]

  ggplot(plot_df, aes(x = torque_norm)) +
    geom_point(aes(y = rms_norm, color = "RMS (normalized)"), alpha = 0.8, size = 1.8) +
    geom_line(aes(y = rms_norm, color = "RMS (normalized)"), linewidth = 0.7) +
    geom_point(aes(y = mdf_scaled, color = "MDF (Hz)"), alpha = 0.8, size = 1.8, shape = 17) +
    geom_line(aes(y = mdf_scaled, color = "MDF (Hz)"), linewidth = 0.7) +
    scale_y_continuous(
      name = "RMS (normalized)",
      sec.axis = sec_axis(
        transform = ~ (. - rms_min) / scale_factor + mdf_min,
        name = "MDF (Hz)"
      )
    ) +
    scale_color_manual(
      values = c("RMS (normalized)" = "#1b6ca8", "MDF (Hz)" = "#d17a22"),
      breaks = c("RMS (normalized)", "MDF (Hz)")
    ) +
    labs(
      title = paste("EX_DYN - RMS and MDF vs normalized torque", ch),
      subtitle = "Right axis is linearly rescaled for display only",
      x = "Normalized torque (MVC_REF = 1)",
      color = NULL
    ) +
    coord_cartesian(ylim = c(rms_min, rms_max)) +
    theme(legend.position = "top")
})

names(ex_dyn_dual_axis_plots) <- unique(ex_dyn_dual_axis_plot_data$channel)

for (plot_obj in ex_dyn_dual_axis_plots) {
  print(plot_obj)
}

ggplot(ex_dyn_mid1s_feature_table_norm,
       aes(x = VC_count, y = rms_norm)) +
  geom_point() +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm") +
  facet_wrap(~ channel) +
  labs(
    title = "EX_DYN - Normalized RMS across repetitions",
    x = "Repetition (VC_count)",
    y = "Normalized RMS"
  )
## `geom_smooth()` using formula = 'y ~ x'

ex_dyn_emg_torque_ratio_trend_plot <- ggplot(
  ex_dyn_emg_torque_ratio_table,
  aes(x = VC_count, y = emg_torque_ratio)
) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.7, size = 1.2) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 0.6) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = FALSE, linewidth = 0.8, color = "#b22222") +
  facet_wrap(~ channel, scales = "free_y") +
  labs(
    title = "EX_DYN - EMG/torque ratio over repetitions",
    subtitle = "EMG/torque ratio = RMS_norm / torque_norm; red line = per-channel linear slope",
    x = "Repetition (VC_count)",
    y = "EMG/torque ratio (RMS_norm / torque_norm)"
  )

print(ex_dyn_emg_torque_ratio_trend_plot)
## `geom_smooth()` using formula = 'y ~ x'

ex_dyn_plot_long <- ex_dyn_mid1s_feature_table_norm |>
  dplyr::select(channel, VC_count, torque_norm, rms_norm, mdf_mid_1s_hz) |>
  tidyr::pivot_longer(
    cols = c(torque_norm, rms_norm, mdf_mid_1s_hz),
    names_to = "metric",
    values_to = "value"
  ) |>
  dplyr::mutate(
    metric = dplyr::recode(
      metric,
      torque_norm = "Normalized torque (MVC_REF = 1)",
      rms_norm = "RMS_norm (a.u.)",
      mdf_mid_1s_hz = "MDF (Hz)"
    ),
    metric = factor(metric, levels = c("Normalized torque (MVC_REF = 1)", "RMS_norm (a.u.)", "MDF (Hz)"))
  )

ggplot(ex_dyn_plot_long, aes(x = VC_count, y = value)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.6, size = 0.8) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = FALSE) +
  facet_grid(metric ~ channel, scales = "free_y") +
  labs(
    title = "EX_DYN - Fatigue progression (middle 1s): normalized torque, RMS_norm, MDF",
    x = "Repetition (VC_count)",
    y = NULL
  )
## `geom_smooth()` using formula = 'y ~ x'

7.3 EX_STA Trends

ex_sta_plot_long <- ex_sta_mid1s_feature_table_norm |>
  dplyr::select(channel, window_id, torque_norm, rms_norm, mdf_mid_1s_hz) |>
  tidyr::pivot_longer(
    cols = c(torque_norm, rms_norm, mdf_mid_1s_hz),
    names_to = "metric",
    values_to = "value"
  ) |>
  dplyr::mutate(
    metric = dplyr::recode(
      metric,
      torque_norm = "Normalized torque (MVC_REF = 1)",
      rms_norm = "RMS_norm (a.u.)",
      mdf_mid_1s_hz = "MDF (Hz)"
    ),
    metric = factor(metric, levels = c("Normalized torque (MVC_REF = 1)", "RMS_norm (a.u.)", "MDF (Hz)"))
  )

ggplot(ex_sta_plot_long, aes(x = window_id, y = value)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.6, size = 0.8) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = FALSE) +
  facet_grid(metric ~ channel, scales = "free_y") +
  labs(
    title = "EX_STA - Fatigue progression (1 s windows)",
    x = "Time (s)",
    y = NULL
  )
## `geom_smooth()` using formula = 'y ~ x'

ex_sta_emg_torque_ratio_trend_plot <- ggplot(
  ex_sta_emg_torque_ratio_table,
  aes(x = window_mid_s, y = emg_torque_ratio)
) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.7, size = 1.2) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 0.6) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = FALSE, linewidth = 0.8, color = "#b22222") +
  facet_wrap(~ channel, scales = "free_y") +
  labs(
    title = "EX_STA - EMG/torque ratio over time",
    subtitle = "EMG/torque ratio = RMS_norm / torque_norm; red line = per-channel linear slope",
    x = "Window midpoint time (s)",
    y = "EMG/torque ratio (RMS_norm / torque_norm)"
  )

print(ex_sta_emg_torque_ratio_trend_plot)
## `geom_smooth()` using formula = 'y ~ x'

7.4 Trend Summary Table

EX_DYN

ex_dyn_summary_table <-
  ex_dyn_mid1s_feature_table_norm |>
  dplyr::group_by(channel) |>
  dplyr::summarise(

    `Normalized torque slope (ratio / rep)` =
      coef(lm(torque_norm ~ VC_count))[2],

    `RMS_norm slope (Δ RMS_norm / rep)` =
      coef(lm(rms_norm ~ VC_count))[2],

    `MDF slope (Hz / rep)` =
      coef(lm(mdf_mid_1s_hz ~ VC_count))[2],

    `RMS_norm vs normalized torque slope (Δ RMS_norm / ratio)` =
      coef(lm(rms_norm ~ torque_norm))[2],

    `MDF vs normalized torque slope (Hz / ratio)` =
      coef(lm(mdf_mid_1s_hz ~ torque_norm))[2],

    .groups = "drop"
  )

ex_dyn_summary_table
## # A tibble: 3 × 6
##   channel Normalized torque slop…¹ RMS_norm slope (Δ RM…² `MDF slope (Hz / rep)`
##   <chr>                      <dbl>                  <dbl>                  <dbl>
## 1 emg10                    -0.0110               -0.00556                 -0.286
## 2 emg6                     -0.0110               -0.00876                 -0.568
## 3 emg8                     -0.0110               -0.00419                 -0.161
## # ℹ abbreviated names: ¹​`Normalized torque slope (ratio / rep)`,
## #   ²​`RMS_norm slope (Δ RMS_norm / rep)`
## # ℹ 2 more variables:
## #   `RMS_norm vs normalized torque slope (Δ RMS_norm / ratio)` <dbl>,
## #   `MDF vs normalized torque slope (Hz / ratio)` <dbl>
